sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 46-year-old female, presents with right shoulder pain that began approximately three weeks ago after a fall from a ladder while painting.
She reports that she was able to continue with her activity after the fall but experienced pain.
She describes the pain as intermittent, rating it as a 6 out of 10 without medication and a 4 out of 10 when taking ibuprofen or Tylenol.
She denies any previous shoulder pain or trauma to that area.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in her arm when she initially hurt it, but it has resolved.
Current medications: The patient has been taking ibuprofen and Tylenol for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in the right shoulder, tenderness to the greater tuberosity of the humerus, and good strong bilateral hand grips.
Neurovascular exam of the right arm shows brisk capillary refill of less than three seconds and equal and bilateral response to sensation and touch.
Test Results: The right shoulder x-ray reveals no fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen as needed for pain, and I recommended Tylenol as an alternative.
I ordered an MRI of the right shoulder to rule out any other conditions.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for six to eight weeks, and I will re-evaluate her in two weeks to determine the need for additional treatment such as a steroid injection.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in two weeks to evaluate her progress and make additional treatment plans.
